text.content:
Book of the Wider World — p.396-397

A few minutes from Stonetop, near the base of the bluff, in the Great Wood (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{200}). Wide, shallow, swift, lined with small stones. Folks come here to wash and fetch water, but rarely alone. It’s not deep, but it’s still a body of water.

  • The current’s constant rush and murmur
  • Flat plain on either side, gravelly, dotted with saplings, weeds, wildflowers
  • Vague sense of dread, of being watched
  • Spring: cold snowmelt swelling the Stream
  • Summer: dragonflies, lightning bugs
  • Spring/Autumn: leaves in current
  • Winter: eerie stillness, icy surface

Tip: raise your voice a little, like you’re talking over background sound

Questions

  • What little ritual do villagers perform every time they cross the Stream? Why?
  • How far have you followed the Stream? Which way? What strange sight did you see on the way?
  • Which of you have encountered the Quiet Twins, the ghostly children who haunt the Stream?
  • What’s the scariest, darkest story you’ve ever heard about the Stream? Who told it to you?
  • Who do you think the Quiet Twins were? Why?

Hooks

  • A dangerous beast takes to watering near town.
  • The Stream runs dry, despite ample rains
  • Something mysterious (dangerous?) is deposited by the current.
  • Haunting songs are heard on misty days; hot-blooded youths are intent on finding the source (an entrancing Fae, like Blush-of-Dawn, page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.YMV8KIodnoZL71Jl]{112}).
  • The Quiet Twins appear more frequently, urgently trying to warn of… something.

Inhabitants

The Quiet Twins
Group, small, spirit, undead, cautious,
unnerving
HP 13; Armor 1 (lacks organs)
Damage icy touch d6 (hand, disadvantage, ignores armor)
Special qualities mute, bound to the Stream
Instinct to warn of some danger (real or not)
  • Watch what happens at the Stream (seen or unseen)
  • Manifest a ghostly form of mist, cold air (harmed only by silver or salt)
  • Mouth and gesture in silent terror
Tip: shrink into your seat, to make yourself
smaller, bug eyed, while mouthing gibberish.
A pair of ghostly children who
haunt the Stream. Sightings
are uncommon, but
common enough.
